page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.6xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY physical cores operating at a base clock speed of 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25%, and single-threaded gzip compression rated as poor. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by average ratings in Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached read/write operations consistently achieve average tier performance, with uncached read bandwidth reaching 96.89 GB/sec.

Given its hardware profile, the ecs.se1ne.6xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high memory capacity rather than raw single-threaded CPU speed. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers like Redis, and multi-threaded data processing. It is less suitable for single-threaded applications, high-performance computing, or workloads requiring local high-speed SSD storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.6xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core memory allocation, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network performance is anchored by a 4 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark data reveals weak single-core CPU performance, though multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth align with average mid-tier standards. This resource distribution makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads. It is highly suited for database hosting, Redis caching layers, and general-purpose memory-bound applications, while being less optimal for single-threaded tasks or high-performance computing.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and high memory density for multi-threaded database and caching workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
